anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge (verses 2.11-2.30). What survives change; what is configuration vs. essence; the imperishable that is the substrate of action.
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The verse anchors on the spec-vs-implementation thread of the chapter thesis: the residual specification (phase three) is the engineering analog of what survives change, and the implementation (phase two) is the configuration. The three-phase structure makes the distinction operationally trackable.

progression_issue.explanation: The verse adds a third independent argument to the do-not-grieve thread (after 2.26 concessive and 2.27 cycle-structural); the engineering layer respects this by adding the perceptual-asymmetry argument rather than restating the previous two.
